diagrams.title: Phong Illumination Model Components
diagrams.description: Shows the three lighting components: ambient (base brightness), diffuse (angle-based), and specular (view-dependent highlight)
diagrams.purpose: Understand how each lighting component contributes to final color

diagrams.title: Shading Methods Comparison
diagrams.description: Side-by-side comparison of Flat, Gouraud, and Phong shading on the same sphere geometry
elements: Flat: Faceted appearance with hard edges
Gouraud: Smooth color gradients but dulled highlights
Phong: Smooth with sharp specular highlights
diagrams.purpose: Visualize differences in shading quality and applications

diagrams.title: Normal Vector and Light Angle
diagrams.description: Shows relationship between surface normal and light direction affecting diffuse intensity
elements: Angle θ between N and L
diagrams.purpose: Understand Lambert's cosine law and diffuse reflection

diagrams.title: Gouraud vs Phong Interpolation
diagrams.description: Shows how intensity interpolation differs from normal interpolation during rasterization
elements: Gouraud: Interpolate I1, I2, I3 intensities
Phong: Interpolate N1, N2, N3 normals, compute I per pixel
diagrams.purpose: Understand the fundamental difference between vertex and fragment shading
